concept: Pour / Cause Material to Flow from a Container
description: To cause liquid or loose material to flow from a container into another place. The vessel's tilt and opening usually begin the pour. A spatial phrase gives its destination. 'selu' (flow) is the material's movement; 'seluto' is the deliberate act that begins and directs it.
sound_symbolism: Fine 'se' flows into the stressed low liquid of 'lu'. Before final 'to', the opening gives 'selu' (flow) in full. That last syllable places a dental stop at the end and gathers the movement into round 'o'.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'mia phialu seluto.' (I pour water.) Source and destination can appear in ordinary spatial phrases, and a vessel may appear with 'roe'. The event-noun rule supplies pouring.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Pouring makes the change of container visible: the hand directs the flow but cannot call back what has already crossed the lip.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A practiced pour saved oil, milk, seed, and water from a spill when every carried measure had cost labor.

tags.activity: directing material out of a container
tags.physical: a controlled flow across the lip of a vessel
tags.spatial: movement from a source container toward a destination
